text: Mappings found inst/mappings folder serve map variables PIAM framework variables needed submission databases. mappings ;-separated files, using # comment character, following mandatory columns: variable: name variable project template unit: unit corresponding variable. IIASA template unit, use unitless: empty cell fail tests avoid unintentially forgetting units. piam_variable: name variable REMIND / MAgPIE / EDGE-T etc. reporting. Never use " ; text. - source: abbreviation PIAM part piam_variable comes . text: Templates often structure ‘parent’ variable sum ‘childs’, Emissions|CO|Energy = Emissions|CO|Energy|Demand + Emissions|CO|Energy|Supply. can checked using summation files placed inst/summations folder. first column contains parent, second childs. want groups single parent, use Emissions|CO2|Energy 2 etc. single-digit number. checkSummations() function allows check data based summation file mapping, example like : can also simply pass IIASA snapshot xlsx csv file checkSummations. starting point new summation file based mapping can generated like : put childs single summation group, split needed remove nonsensical ones. REMIND, summation checks can performed automatically every run added checkProjectSummations.R.
code: d <- generateIIASASubmission("remind.mif", mapping = "ScenarioMIP", outputDirectory = NULL, checkSummation = FALSE) checkSummations(d, template = "ScenarioMIP", summationsFile = "ScenarioMIP", outputDirectory = NULL) library(tidyverse); library(piamInterfaces) vars <- grep("^Lifetime|^Price|^OM Cost|^Capital Cost", unique(piamInterfaces::getMapping("ScenarioMIP")$variable), invert = TRUE, value = TRUE) liste <- mip::extractVariableGroups(vars) tbl <- tibble() for (l in sort(names(liste))) {   childs <- grep("\\*$", liste[[l]], invert = TRUE, value = TRUE)   if (length(childs) > 1) {     tbl <- rbind(tbl, tibble(parent = l, child = childs, factor = 1), tibble(parent = "", child = "", factor = ""))   } } writeMapping(tbl, "summation_group_ScenarioMIP.csv")

title: Retrieves all variables allocated to source potentially used in mappings to project variables — getMappingVariables
text: Falk Benke, Oliver Richters

title: Tutorial
text: Mappings found inst/mappings folder serve map variables PIAM framework variables needed submission databases. mappings ;-separated files, using # comment character, following mandatory columns: variable: name variable project template unit: unit corresponding variable. IIASA template unit, use unitless: empty cell fail tests avoid unintentially forgetting units. piam_variable: name variable REMIND / MAgPIE / EDGE-T etc. reporting. Please use |+| notation try remain consistent REMIND mif facilitate debugging humans, technical point view completely irrelevant. can use updatePlusUnit() update correct |+| spelling unit based model data file. piam_unit: unit corresponding piam_variable piam_factor: factor piam_variable multiplied units match Recommended columns: - description: description text defining variable. Never use " ; text. - source: abbreviation PIAM part piam_variable comes . Use B = Brick, C = MAGICC, M = MAgPIE, R = REMIND, S = SDP postprocessing, T = EDGE-Transport. column used select variables passed remind2 coupling tests. variable normally reported, add small x model abbreviation skipped tests. New source abbreviations explained must added inst/sources.csv. Additionally, mappings use columns: - idx: serial number variable - tier: importance variable, 1 important - comment: place internal comments - interpolation: sets interpolation method variable (.e. piam_variable) (currently supports linear). set linear, adds yearly values 2005 2100 linear interpolation selected output variables. - piam_weight: calculates weighted average multiple entries piam_variable. Provide different piam_variable column, generateIIASASubmission() split data rows contain weight pointers, resolve weights numerical values (via join operation submission input data) modify value based weighting. takes place private .resolveWeights method.

text: Templates often structure ‘parent’ variable sum ‘childs’, Emissions|CO|Energy = Emissions|CO|Energy|Demand + Emissions|CO|Energy|Supply. can checked using summation files placed inst/summations folder. first column contains parent, second childs. want groups single parent, use Emissions|CO2|Energy 2 etc. single-digit number. checkSummations() function allows check data based summation file mapping, example like : can also simply pass IIASA snapshot xlsx csv file checkSummations. starting point new summation file based mapping can generated like : put childs single summation group, split needed remove nonsensical ones. REMIND, summation checks can performed automatically every run added checkProjectSummations.R.
code: d <- generateIIASASubmission("remind.mif", mapping = "ScenarioMIP", outputDirectory = NULL, checkSummation = FALSE) checkSummations(d, template = "ScenarioMIP", summationsFile = "ScenarioMIP", outputDirectory = NULL) library(tidyverse); library(piamInterfaces) vars <- grep("^Lifetime|^Price|^OM Cost|^Capital Cost", unique(piamInterfaces::getMapping("ScenarioMIP")$variable), invert = TRUE, value = TRUE) liste <- mip::extractVariableGroups(vars) tbl <- tibble() for (l in sort(names(liste))) {   childs <- grep("\\*$", liste[[l]], invert = TRUE, value = TRUE)   if (length(childs) > 1) {     tbl <- rbind(tbl, tibble(parent = l, child = childs, factor = 1), tibble(parent = "", child = "", factor = ""))   } } writeMapping(tbl, "summation_group_ScenarioMIP.csv")
